title: Megyathus acetabulosum
taxonomy: Megyathus acetabulosum is classified as a species of extinct bryozoan, a type of marine invertebrate. Bryozoans are colonial animals that belong to the phylum Bryozoa. They are typically found in ancient fossil records, particularly those from the Paleozoic and Mesozoic eras. The classification of Megyathus acetabulosum reflects its unique characteristics and its position within the broader group of bryozoans. Further study of its morphology and geological context can provide more insight into its classification.
parts: Megyathus acetabulosum is a fossilized species of extinct genus. Unfortunately, I don't have any information about its medicinal parts.
